folio.label: 139v
folio.l: Whicú is ful ¹ilous , and hatú be ©oore
For al to sone , moun they lerne loore
Of boldnesse , whan she woxen is a wyf
¢ And ©e Maistresses , in ©oure olde lyf
That lordes dougútres , han in gouänaunce
Ne taketú of my wordes , no displesaunce
Thenketú that ©e ben set , in gouernynges
Of lordes dougútres , oonly for two thynges
Outher for ©e han kept , ©oure honeste
Or elles ©e han falle , in freelete
And knowen wel I now , the olde daunce
And han forsaken fully , swicú meschaunce
For euäe moo , therfore for cristes sake
To teche hem vertu , loke that ©e ne slake
¢ A thef of venysou~ , that hatú for laft
His likerousnesse , and al his olde craft
Kan kepe a forest , best of any man
Now kepetú wel , for if ©e wole ©e kan
Loke wel that ©e , vn to no vice assente
Leest ©e be dampned , for ©owre wykke entente
For who so dotú , a traytour is certeyn
And taketú kepe , of that that I shal seyn
Of alle tresou~ , souäayn pestilence
Is whan a wygút bitraisetú , Innocence
ñe fadres and ©e modres , eke also
Thougú ©e han children , be it on or moo
ñoure is the charge , of al hirö såueau~ce
Whil that they ben , vnder ©oure gouänaunce
Betú war , if be ensaumple of ©oure lyuynge
Or by ©oure necligence , in chastisynge
That they ¹isshe , for I dar wel seye
If that they doon , ©e shul it deere a beye
Vnder a Shepherde , soft and necligent
The wolf , hatú many a sheep and lamb to rent
Suffisetú on ensample , now as heere
For I moot turne a geyn , to my matere
¢ This mayde , of whicú , I wol this tale expresse
So kept hirö self , hir neded no Maistresse
For in hirö lyuyngö , maydens mygúten rede
As in a book , euäy good word , or dede
That longetú to a mayden , vertuous
She was so prudent , and so bountevous
For whicú the fame out sprongö , on euäy syde
